code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test05-MAY-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly low serum glucose and elevated creatinine, suggesting possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria, nitrites, or leukocytes. These findings may indicate renal tubular dysfunction (e.g., early diabetic nephropathy or other renal pathology). Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
